Mary Guirguis, Esq. Downtown LA Law Group 216-389-3765 ATTQRNEY FQR Plaintiff Veronica Jones
Jennifer J. Capabianco, Esq., HAWKINS PARNELL & YOUNG LLP (415) 979-2012 jcapabianco@hpylaw.com Attorney for Defendant MHDC BETEL LP

Hearing held. discussed, not argued. Court rules as follows

PLAINTIFF VERONICA JONES AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Motion is granted.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MHDC BETEL LP's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No opposition was received to the court's tentative ruling by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person, in compliance with instructions stated within the tentative ruling. Therefore, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's motion to compel responses to Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Request for Production of Documents, Set One, and sanctions, is granted.

Plaintiff has not responded to the discovery. Plaintiff's counsel opposed relief because of a breakdown in the attorney-client relationship and a lack of communication. The court continued the matter on counsel's request to permit counsel to seek to withdraw. Counsel has not filed any motion to withdraw.

Discovery is an obligation of bringing a case and defendant has waited a considerable time. The court orders plaintiff to provide verified, objection-free responses within 30 days of entry of this order. The court does not find substantial justification or unjust circumstances and orders plaintiff to pay $787.50 to defendant within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-05-2025 Continued To Dec-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MHDC BETEL LP's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Grant Plaintiff's attorney's request to continue the hearing on the merits of Defendant's discovery motion to provide her an opportunity to obtain a ruling on a motion to be relieved as Plaintiff's attorney of record. Counsel's motion should be filed promptly to avoid further prejudice to Defendant. The court suggests that Defendant's discovery motion be continued to December 10, 2025, or other mutually agreed upon date in December 2025.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CM)
